findings.title: Untrusted-input to privileged-action path
findings.rationale: Untrusted input surfaces can reach high-impact tools with no injection-mediation guardrail.
findings.remediation: Add an injection-mediation guardrail, or require human approval on every high-impact tool reachable from an untrusted surface, or isolate untrusted input from privileged actions.

findings.title: Injection-persistent memory
findings.rationale: Untrusted content persists in memory and is re-fed to the prompt while high-impact tools are enabled.
findings.remediation: Do not persist untrusted content into prompt-feeding memory, or sanitize/quarantine it before reuse.

findings.title: Autonomous high-impact tool without human approval
findings.rationale: An autonomous agent holds a high-impact tool with no human-approval gate.
findings.remediation: Add a human-approval gate on high-impact tools, or reduce agent autonomy to human-in-the-loop.

findings.title: Over-broad tool scope (least privilege)
findings.rationale: One or more tools are granted a scope broader than the declared purpose.
findings.remediation: Narrow each tool grant to the minimum scope the declared purpose requires.

findings.title: No output guardrail/DLP for sensitive data class
findings.rationale: The agent handles a sensitive data class with no output filter or DLP guardrail.
findings.remediation: Enable an output filter / DLP guardrail appropriate to the data classification handled.

findings.title: No prohibited-behavior guardrail for a declared surface
findings.rationale: A prohibited-behavior surface is reachable with no refusal guardrail configured.
findings.remediation: Configure a refusal/guardrail for each declared prohibited surface (e.g., financial/legal/tax advice, money movement).

findings.title: No instruction-source boundary
findings.rationale: The system prompt does not separate trusted instructions from untrusted retrieved/tool content.
findings.remediation: State in the system prompt that tool/retrieved/user content is data, not instructions, and must not be executed.

findings.title: No fail-closed / escalation on uncertainty
findings.rationale: The failure mode does not fail closed and/or does not escalate to a human on uncertainty.
findings.remediation: Configure the agent to fail closed and escalate to a human when identity, source, or authorization is uncertain.

findings.title: No evaluation/benchmark coverage
findings.rationale: No evaluation harness is wired for this agent.
findings.remediation: Wire an evaluation/benchmark suite (route to ai-evaluation-benchmark-builder) before deployment.

findings.title: Insufficient tool-call audit logging
findings.rationale: Tool calls are not logged, limiting auditability and incident response.
findings.remediation: Enable durable audit logging of every tool call with inputs, outputs, and identity.

adjudication_note: Recommendation only; requires human adjudication before any deployment decision. No approval, risk acceptance, attestation, or review closure has been recorded.
disclaimer: Risk review evidence and recommendations only; not an approval, risk acceptance, or attestation. Deployment requires human adjudication by the accountable AI risk owner.
narrative: Risk review of agent collections-outreach-agent (catalog agent-controls-2026.07) fired 10 controls: 1 Critical, 4 High, 4 Moderate, 1 Low. The Critical finding (C-INJ-01) is that untrusted web and free-form input can reach an autonomous email.send and payment.plan.write path with no injection-mediation guardrail. Recommended risk rating: Critical. Recommended disposition: Remediate-before-deploy. These are findings and recommendations for the accountable AI risk owner; the deployment decision requires human adjudication and is out of scope for this skill.
notes: Every fired finding cites the exact configuration locus that triggered it.
